Official name (+source): Greenjoy Company Limited (Business Registration Certificate, Tax Code 0315318999) — greenjoystraw.com/en
Est. company size: Small/medium manufacturer; ~100 hectares partner-farmer grass cultivation area (Equator Initiative, 2024). Exact headcount not publicly available.
Primary customers: Horeca (hotels/restaurants/cafes) — 1,000+ in Vietnam; consumers via Amazon/Shopee/Tiki; export B2B to international hospitality supply chains
Year founded: 2018 per Business Registration Certificate (Tax Code 0315318999, issued 10 Oct 2018) — greenjoystraw.com; 2019 per Equator Initiative/UNDP profile (2024) — conflicting sources, see notes
Ownership status: Private company, founder-led (CEO Vo Quoc Thao Nguyen) — greenjoystraw.com/en/ve-chung-toi
Core model: Manufactures compostable straws/utensils from Mekong Delta grass (Lepironia/grey sedge); D2C e-commerce + B2B Horeca supply; ~80% of production exported to 30+ countries
Geographic scope: Vietnam (Long An, Mekong Delta production); exports to 30+ countries incl. US, Germany, France, Belgium, Poland, Japan, South Korea — equatorinitiative.org
Categories: Consumer Brand; Packaging Manufacturer; compostable food-service dining ware
Key partners / customers: Amazon, Shopee, Tiki (distribution); Mekong Delta farmer cooperatives and commune committees (raw material supply); F&B distributors — equatorinitiative.org
Impact metrics: 40 million plastic straws replaced (both sources); plastic mass reduced: 20 tons (official site) vs 250 tons (Equator Initiative/UNDP, 2024); CO2 reduced: 50 tons (official site) vs 200 tons (Equator Initiative); no independently audited figures found
Verification notes: Founding year conflict: official Business Registration Certificate shows 2018; Equator Initiative/UNDP profile (2024) states 2019 — treated 2018 as higher-confidence given it is a registry-backed record. Impact metrics conflict materially between official site and UNDP-affiliated Equator Initiative profile (20 vs 250 tons plastic; 50 vs 200 tons CO2) for what appears to be the same cumulative claim — both undated to a specific measurement year, neither independently audited. Recommend future verification via a formally published impact/annual report if one exists.
